LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAMES VLAHOS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFFS ESTABLISH A PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFFS WILL PREVAIL ON THEIR CLAIM. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFFS ESTABLISH A PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFFS WILL PREVAIL ON THEIR CLAIM. THE COURT RULES ON PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT SUSTAINED PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AND SECOND OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE; THE COURT OVERRULES PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AND FOURTH, OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE. THE COURT RULES ON DEFENDANT PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC'S OBJECTIONS AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT OBJECTIONS TO FOX'S DECLARATION, EXCEPT, THE COURT SUSTAINS THE NINTH OBJECTION TO FOX'S DECLARATION; THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MCCAULEY DECLARATION, EXCEPT THE COURT SUSTAINS THE SECOND OBJECTION; THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MUELLER DECLARATION, EXCEPT THE COURT SUSTAINS THE SECOND OBJECTION. THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO HEAD DECLARATION, EXCEPT THE COURT SUSTAINS THE SECOND OBJECTION; THE COURT OVERRULES DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO BECK DECLARATION, EXCEPT, THE COURT SUSTAINS THE SECOND OBJECTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JAMES VLAHOS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Raquel Fox, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic, (415) 771-9850 x115, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Mark J. Romeo, Esq., Law Offices of Mark J. Romeo, (415) 395-9315, appearing for Defendant, James Vlahos. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion Denied. Complaint arises from protected activity. Plaintiffs establish a probability that plaintiffs will prevail on their claim. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PRANA EIGHT PROPERTIES, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Raquel Fox, Esq. of Tenderloin Housing Clinic, (415) 771-9850 x 115, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Andrew M. Zacks, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht, (415) 956-8100, appearing for Defendant Prana Eight Properties LLC, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion Denied. Complaint arises from protected activity. Plaintiffs establish a probability that plaintiffs will prevail on their claim. The Court rules on plaintiff's evidentiary objections as follows: The Court sustained plaintiff's first and second objections to defendant's evidence; the Court overrules plaintiff's third and fourth, objections to defendant's evidence. The Court rules on Defendant Prana Eight Properties, LLC'S objections as follows: The Court overrules Defendant objections to Fox's Declaration, except, the Court sustains the ninth objection to Fox's Declaration; The Court overrules defendant's objections to McCauley Declaration, except the Court sustains the second objection; The Court overrules defendant's objections to Mueller Declaration, except the Court sustains the second objection. The Court overrules defendant's objections to Head Declaration, except the Court sustains the second objection; The Court overrules defendant's objections to Beck Declaration, except, the Court sustains the second objection. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
